title: Confounding by Indication Explained
script_summary: This script explains confounding by indication in a clear and engaging manner.

segments.voiceover: Science has a clearer story about confounding by indication than social media does.
Confounding by indication happens when the reason for treatment affects the outcome, misleading our understanding of effectiveness.
For example, if sicker patients receive a drug, it might seem less effective, but it's not the drug's fault.
Researchers use statistical methods to adjust for this bias, but uncertainty remains.
Understanding confounding by indication helps us make better health choices and interpret studies accurately.
Want more insights? Hit follow for clarity in health and science!
